quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of violent threats moved over the board this morning, centered around a post from an anon who claimed they would kill BJP and Congress members if they were in R&AW. This sparked a wide debate on the economy and ideology.

A front of class resentment pushed in from the east, bringing with it accusations of privilege and counter-accusations of poverty. One anon pointed out that a public figure's educational background and career choices were evidence of their dedication to their country.

A scattered drizzle of local news also touched down, including a report from an Amravati resident of a murder near their house, possibly gang-related.

Elsewhere, a brief but intense high-pressure system of libertarian ideology flared up, with one anon calling for a capitalist system and the abolition of reservations.

articles.title: Anon threatens to kill BJP and Congress members, sparks debate on economy and ideology
articles.body:
Anon posted a threat to kill all BJP and Congress members if they were in R&AW, then move on to other activities to improve the nation.

Another anon responded, discussing energy sovereignty and geopolitical situations, suggesting India should align with Russia but noting the economy and political class can't handle it.

A different anon brought up the topic of socialism and communism, stating that while communism is a virus that needs to be eradicated, socialism is cool.

They suggested India follow a capitalist model like America's 1950s-70s, with a 90% tax rate. Anon also debated the merits of ethanol as an energy source, with one poster arguing it's a viable long-term option for energy needs.

Another anon countered that ethanol still causes pollution and that hydrogen is a better green fuel. The conversation turned to ideologies, with an anon stating they are flexible and think capitalism can be used to simulate socialism.

They also mentioned that Amazon's production line was inspired by Soviet-era cybernetics. Anon discussed the practicality of hydrogen as a fuel source, pointing out the difficulties in storing and transporting it.

One poster argued that the energy released by hydrogen is low compared to diesel, and that it requires expensive and complex storage solutions.

The thread also touched on politics, with an anon criticizing the government for not improving per capita income and for allowing welfare schemes that waste resources.

Another poster defended Modi, saying the difference between him and Congress leaders is that Modi gives money to industrialists to generate jobs, while old Congress leaders use it to create government jobs.

Anon also exchanged views on socialism and communism, with one poster claiming that elites are spreading socialism to import third-worlders and dilute local nationalists.

Another anon argued that socialism and UBI are opium for the masses to forget about their role in society as elites will not need them anymore with automation.

articles.title: Indiachan /b/ Thread: Debating Energy, Politics, and Nationalism
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion by criticizing another poster, calling them 'buck broke pajeet' and accusing them of not standing up against a corrupt system. The original post also mentioned someone who was born rich but chose to work for their homeland.

Another anon responded, accusing 'chamar chodu' of distorting facts and comparing the rate of electrification under the UPA and NDA governments. This anon provided an example of how even with lower total electricity, the UPA had a higher growth rate in electrification.

A different anon chimed in, stating that large parts of India didn't have toilets or electricity before 2014 and that the INC was responsible for keeping India in a state of underdevelopment.

This poster also mentioned that India still has the same energy reserves. One anon discussed the rate of terrorism during Manmohan Singh's era, stating that it had declined sharply. They also mentioned that the UPA had more deportations than the NDA.

The conversation also touched on topics like energy sovereignty, with one anon stating that India is currently energy-safe and that Modi is working to improve infrastructure.

Another anon disagreed, saying that Modi is not doing it the right way and is prioritizing profit for certain groups.

The discussion also involved arguments about nationalism, with one anon stating that nationalism can keep a country together for a while when the economy stagnates. Another anon mentioned that India will either disintegrate or become a stratocracy.

The thread also included personal attacks and insults, with multiple anons exchanging barbs and accusations.

articles.title: Amravati resident reports murder near their house
articles.body:
A murder was reported near the house of Yaarrronnn, who posted on the board about the incident. The victim, known to Yaarrronnn as 'k2a' and often seen with a Honda Sherni, was allegedly killed in gang violence, though the exact motive is unclear.

Anon respondents weighed in on the topic, with one suggesting that the victim's behavior, described as trying to appear powerful, may have contributed to his death.

Another anon mentioned that 'k2wa gangs are very common' and that their presence has decreased in their area, possibly due to stricter police action.

The discussion also touched on the demographics of Amravati, with Yaarrronnn noting that the Muslim population in the area has doubled since 2011 and that they have 'completely taken over the streets.' Anons discussed the relationship between local populations and gang violence, with some making general claims about the behavior of certain groups.

Some respondents shared links to social media profiles, including an Instagram account allegedly linked to the victim, and made observations about the victim's online presence and associations. Yaarrronnn also shared a personal anecdote about having quarrels with someone in the past.

The conversation included speculation about the circumstances of the murder and comments about social dynamics in the area, with some anons making assertions about the behavior and influence of specific groups.

articles.title: Anon Wants Libertarian India
articles.body:
Anon posted about their desire for a libertarian system in India, like Europe, and an end to reservations and freebies. They suggested tax money should only go to defense, education, and development.

Another anon responded that a merit-based system would be nepotistic, citing Bengaluru's tech capital status as an example of proximity to wealth rather than merit.

A third anon chimed in, saying Europe also has systems like reservations and inflation adjustments, and mocked those who complain about India's state while living in slums.

Anon argued that some individuals from lower backgrounds excel in exams, while wealthy ones may not be talented. Others exchanged insults, with one anon accusing another of being a "shit eater" and a "pajeet".

articles.title: Anon Expresses Support for Wangchuk
articles.body:
Anon posted 'I dont care what any of you bjp cucks say but i stand with wangchuk. Or how many leftists troon support him.

He is right here.' Another anon replied, 'Wangcuck is genuinely a good man but his efforts are wasted on a shithole and shittier people of this country.' A different anon stated, 'cant wait until its revelaed hes a child molestor..its obvious just from the way he speaks.' One respondent asked, 'Are you a faggot why so obssessed with sucking dicks.'
